name: Isle of the Blessed
description: The Isle of the Blessed, also known as the Elysian Fields or Elysium, is a paradisiacal realm in Greek mythology reserved for mortals chosen by the gods for their exceptional virtue, heroism, or favor. It is a place of eternal spring, where the sun always shines, and the air is filled with sweet fragrances. Unlike the Underworld, which is the common destination for all souls, the Isle of the Blessed is a special reward, a haven from suffering and death. Inhabitants enjoy a life of leisure, partaking in feasts, games, and the company of other heroes and virtuous individuals. Some accounts describe it as being ruled by Cronus, after his release from Tartarus, while others place it under the dominion of Rhadamanthus. Access to the Isle of the Blessed was not automatic; it required divine intervention or exceptional merit, making it a symbol of ultimate reward and a testament to the gods' appreciation of mortal excellence. The location of the Isle of the Blessed varied in different accounts, sometimes placed in the far west, beyond the Pillars of Heracles, or in the heavens. It represents the ultimate afterlife reward in Greek belief, a serene and joyous existence far removed from the trials and tribulations of mortal life, and a place where heroes and the righteous could find eternal peace and happiness.
subtitle: Paradise for heroes and the virtuous.

keyMyths.title: Achilles' Choice
keyMyths.description: In some versions, Achilles is offered immortality on the Isle of the Blessed but chooses a short, glorious life and heroic death instead, highlighting the allure and sacrifice associated with this realm.

keyMyths.title: Menelaus' Journey
keyMyths.description: Menelaus, husband of Helen, was promised passage to the Elysian Fields by Proteus, as he was a son-in-law of Zeus, demonstrating the role of divine lineage in gaining access to the Isle of the Blessed.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: Provides early descriptions of Elysium and the criteria for entering.
sources.text: Provides early descriptions of Elysium and the criteria for entering.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: Mentions the Isle of the Blessed as a reward for righteous heroes.
